1960s.title: Oil state formation and administered prices
1960s.body: Iraq's early CPI record reflects an oil-backed state economy where public spending, food supply, and administered prices shaped household costs.
1960s.driver: Public spending, food imports, administered prices
1960s.outcome: The dinar route starts before nationalization, wars, sanctions, and currency reform.
1960s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

1970s.title: Oil nationalization and spending surge
1970s.body: Oil nationalization and the 1970s oil boom expanded state revenue and public spending before later conflict transformed the economy.
1970s.outcome: The route includes the 1970s, but later 1979-1989 CPI rows are missing in the source and are not interpolated.

1990s.title: Sanctions and currency collapse
1990s.body: After the Gulf War, sanctions, fiscal crisis, and currency weakness reshaped purchasing power in ways far beyond ordinary inflation cycles.
1990s.event: Gulf War aftermath and UN sanctions
1990s.outcome: Official CPI is a narrow window into a much deeper purchasing-power shock.
1990s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

2000s.title: War, currency reform, and reconstruction
2000s.body: The 2003 invasion, new Iraqi dinar issuance, security disruption, and reconstruction changed prices through supply, wages, and public finance.
2000s.driver: Security, imports, public wages, currency reform
2000s.outcome: The decade's CPI movement sits inside a broader institutional reset.

2010s.title: Oil prices, conflict, and fiscal pressure
2010s.body: Oil-price swings, the conflict with ISIS, displacement, and public-sector spending pressures affected prices and household resilience.
2010s.driver: Oil revenue, security disruption, food and housing costs
2010s.outcome: Inflation was tied to both conflict logistics and oil-funded public finance.
2010s.evidence: World Bank / IMF IFS annual CPI

2020s.title: Devaluation and food-price pressure
2020s.body: The early 2020s brought oil-market swings, a dinar devaluation, and global food-price pressure into household budgets.
2020s.event: 2020 oil shock and dinar devaluation period
2020s.driver: Oil revenue, exchange rate, food imports
2020s.outcome: The 2024 endpoint reflects a post-shock economy still tied to oil and import prices.
